title: Dark Loom swarm dashboard
acceptance: Dashboard loads from a run, collection, or continuation artifact
Overview presents the current goal, progress percentage, daily contribution chart, and token cost metrics without lane-level clutter
Daily contribution dots show an immediate hover popover with date, activity, done, and event counts, including gap-tolerant nearest-dot hover behavior
Board tab presents AI-managed tasks in opinionated read-only columns including Backlog, To do, Active, Review, Ready, Done, and Blocked
Board content is horizontally scrollable while each column is vertically scrollable
Board cards expose stable copyable ticket IDs
Clicking a board card opens a read-only task detail dialog with higher-level summary, status, reasons, expandable per-file diffs, results, and evidence
Diffs render as structured color-coded rows with old/new line numbers and lightweight syntax token highlighting
Changed-file and evidence path names reveal the local file or folder in Finder through a bounded artifact endpoint without an embedded artifact viewer

Swarm tab shows only active agents, using stable generated description-plus-animal names, compact identity colors, model labels, current ticket assignments, token usage, touched files, and evidence artifacts
Performance tab presents chart-first token, runtime, cache-hit, waste, and optimization-signal views over time without adding steering controls
Tab and ticket state is deep-linked in the URL hash so refresh preserves the selected tab and open task dialog
Questions tab presents only agent questions that need human answers and supports copying question codes without extra explanatory copy
Dashboard updates from a server-sent event stream with a polling fallback for runtimes without EventSource
The UI service is read-only: users steer swarms by talking to Codex, not by posting dashboard commands
Dark mode is the default and only shipped theme
